svg.aria-label: The orchestrator-worker pattern, also called the supervisor. A LEAD agent at the top decomposes one task, Prepare a customer briefing on refunds, the earbuds warranty, and shipping, into three typed briefs. Each typed brief carries an objective, an output format, tool guidance, and boundaries. The lead fans out to three WORKERS. Each worker is the Part 1 agent reused as a black box and runs in its OWN isolated context of 14 tokens, seeing only its brief. Worker w1 summarizes the refund policy and returns Refunds within 30 days, a 10 percent restocking fee applies after the window. Worker w2 states the earbuds warranty and returns The Globex earbuds carry a 2-year limited warranty. Worker w3 lists the shipping options and returns Standard shipping is 3 to 5 business days, express is next business day. All three post their result to a shared append-only BLACKBOARD. The lead then reads the blackboard and SYNTHESIZES the three entries into one briefing. The contexts are isolated, and with real concurrency the independent workers run in one round.
svg.title: The orchestrator-worker (supervisor) pattern: decompose into typed briefs, fan out to isolated workers, post to a blackboard, synthesize

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

✓ LEAD SYNTHESIZES the blackboard into one briefing
Refunds within 30 days; a 10% restocking fee applies after the window. The Globex earbuds carry a 2-year limited warranty. Standard shipping is 3 to 5 business days; express is next business day.
Isolated contexts; independent workers run in one round with real concurrency. Offline they run sequentially, so we report LLM-call count and token isolation, never wall-clock.
